description_long: This is a vintage, cylindrical foam fire extinguisher with a weathered red exterior, showing signs of rust and wear. The body is made of metal and features faded white instructional text and a pictogram of a fire, indicating its purpose. A black rubber hose is attached to the top, looping down and secured to the side, with a metal nozzle at the end. The extinguisher has a prominent metal handle and lever mechanism at the top for operation, and a sturdy black base with two metal support brackets for stability. The overall design is utilitarian, with visible bolts and seams, and the surface is rough and matte due to age and exposure. This type of extinguisher would typically be found in garages, industrial areas, or public urban spaces.
description: A weathered red metal foam fire extinguisher with a black hose, metal handle, and faded white instructional markings. The surface is rough and rusted, with a sturdy base and visible support brackets.
description_view_0: The extinguisher is seen from the back, showing the full length of the black hose secured vertically and the metal support brackets at the base.
description_view_1: This angled view highlights the handle, lever, and the hose curving outward, as well as the faded white pictogram and text on the front.
description_view_2: The rear side is visible, displaying more of the worn red surface, faded text, and the symmetrical support brackets at the base.
description_view_3: A front-angled view showing the handle, lever, and the hose extending outward, with the pictogram and text most clearly visible.
attribute_label_or_signage: Faded white instructional text and a pictogram of a fire are visible on the front and sides of the cylinder.
attribute_mounting_bracket_or_holder: Two metal support brackets are attached to the base, forming a floor stand.
attribute_hose_or_nozzle: A black rubber hose is attached to the top, looping down the side, with a metal nozzle at the end.
